summary: Russian forces reported multiple reactive UAV (Shahed) attacks across central and northern Ukraine, including near Kirovohrad, Dnipropetrovsk, and Chernihiv regions. Ukraine's Main Intelligence Directorate disclosed deployment of new small‑range cruise missiles Banderol and Dan with up to 500 km range. Intense air activity continued with over 50 drones intercepted near Moscow and sustained combat in the Sumy and Zaporozhye fronts.
key_points: Reactive Shahed UAVs were observed approaching Kirovohrad, Dnipropetrovsk, and Chernihiv areas from various directions (Source: @eRadarrua/98822).
Ukraine reported the fielding of new cruise missiles named Banderol and Dan, capable of 500 km range and 150 kg warheads (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31273).
More than 50 Ukrainian drones were shot down during a night‑time raid on the approach to Moscow (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Four Tu‑95MS bombers took off from the Ukrainianka airfield, likely heading to Engels (Source: @eRadarrua/98842).
Ukrainian forces claimed destruction of enemy personnel and equipment near Novoivanivka using 'drone‑watcher' systems (Source: @DeepStateUA/23603).
Multiple Shahed attacks were reported near Kropyvnytskyi railway station and surrounding districts (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68178).
Russian tactical aviation activity was noted on southeastern and northern directions (Source: @kpszsu/66941).
Ukrainian authorities signed a Gripen fighter jet procurement and aid agreement with Sweden (Source: @UkraineNow/82300).

sections.executive_summary: Russian Shahed UAV attacks intensified across central Ukraine, striking near Kirovohrad, Dnipropetrovsk, and Chernihiv. Ukraine introduced new Banderol and Dan cruise missiles, extending its strike capability up to 500 km. Air operations remained heavy, with over 50 drones downed near Moscow and continued combat in Sumy and Zaporozhye.

points: Ukrainian forces reported reactive Shahed UAVs approaching Kirovohrad from the west near Dolynska and Kropyvnytskyi, prompting air defence alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/98839).
In Dnipropetrovsk region, Shahed drones were observed flying west past Solone and north‑west past Shyroke, indicating multiple ingress routes (Source: @kpszsu/66925; @kpszsu/66928).
Chernihiv sector saw reactive UAVs moving south past Borzna and north of Borzna, highlighting pressure on northern front lines (Source: @kpszsu/66947; @eRadarrua/98832).
Ukrainian Main Intelligence Directorate disclosed deployment of Banderol and Dan cruise missiles with up to 500 km range and 150 kg warheads, enhancing strike depth (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31273).
Ukrainian 'drone‑watcher' units of the 67th OMBR "Iron Falcons" engaged enemy personnel and equipment near Novoivanivka (Source: @DeepStateUA/23603).
Ukraine signed a Gripen fighter jet procurement and aid package with Sweden for 16 Gripen E and 16 Gripen C/D, with deliveries slated for 2029 (Source: @UkraineNow/82300).
Ukrainian diplomatic outreach includes discussions with France on licensed production of SCALP missiles and consultations with the US on similar capabilities (Source: @UkraineNow/82283).
Four Tu‑95MS strategic bombers took off from the Ukrainianka airfield, likely en route to Engels, indicating strategic bomber activity (Source: @eRadarrua/98842).
Russian tactical aviation conducted operations on southeastern and northern axes, employing aerial strike assets (Source: @kpszsu/66941).
Russian forces reported intensive drone activity, with more than 50 UAVs intercepted near Moscow during a night raid (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).

Russian troops engaged in heavy fighting in Sumy direction, with Su‑25 aircraft of the North Guards Motor Rifle Brigade supporting ground assaults in Bachivsk and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
In the Belgorod region, civilian casualties were recorded from drone‑detonated shrapnel and barotrauma incidents (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian forces advanced westward on the Slavyansk direction, aiming to close encirclement at Orekhovatka (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian authorities closed multiple railway crossing points on the borders with Latvia, Finland, and Estonia, affecting logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82301).
Ukrainian reports indicated Shahed UAVs approaching Zaporizhzhia from the east, threatening the city and surrounding settlements (Source: @kpszsu/66930).
Combat in the Zaporozhye region intensified, with Ukrainian forces liberating Novoselovka and pushing west toward Yegorovka and Omelnik (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian air strikes targeted the Sumy direction, with Su‑25 support in Shostka district and ongoing firefights in Pisarevka, Marino, Nova Sich, and Khoten (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Ukrainian units of the 36th Guards Motor Rifle Brigade captured the settlement Bogodarivka in Dnipropetrovsk region, facing counter‑attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian forces continued assaults on Krasnaya Liman, fighting for each house amid a broader summer offensive (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Ukrainian air defence reported downing of multiple Shahed drones over the eastern front, including near Kharkiv and Volchansk sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Shahed UAVs were observed over Chernihiv region, moving south past Borzna and north of Borzna, indicating pressure on northern supply lines (Source: @kpszsu/66947; @eRadarrua/98832).
Ukrainian reports noted a reactive UAV heading toward Kryvyi Rih from the south, suggesting southern approaches to the northern industrial corridor (Source: @kpszsu/66929).
Russian tactical aviation activity was reported on the northern direction, employing strike aircraft against Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/66941).
Ukrainian forces engaged enemy elements near Novoivanivka, a location in the north‑central sector, using drone‑watcher systems (Source: @DeepStateUA/23603).
Russian forces maintained artillery and drone pressure on villages in the Kharkiv direction, including Losevka and Zemlyany Yar (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Ukrainian air defence intercepted multiple UAVs over the northern front, preventing further incursions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Shahed attacks were reported near Kropyvnytskyi railway station, with multiple reactive drones observed from the south (Source: @mon1tor_ua/68178; @mon1tor_ua/68179; @mon1tor_ua/68184).
Ukrainian forces reported reactive UAVs near the Dnipro region, flying west past Solone and north‑west past Shyroke (Source: @kpszsu/66925; @kpszsu/66928).
Russian advances were noted toward the Oskol River in the south, though at a slower pace than publicly claimed (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Ukrainian air defence engaged Shahed drones targeting southern settlements, including in the Belgorod border area (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian tactical aviation conducted strikes on the southeastern direction, supporting ground operations (Source: @kpszsu/66941).
Civilian casualties from drone attacks were reported in the Belgorod region, including shrapnel injuries (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
More than 50 Ukrainian drones were shot down during a night‑time raid on the approach to Moscow, indicating heightened aerial contest (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian tactical aviation operated on southeastern and northern axes, employing strike aircraft against Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/66941).
Four Tu‑95MS strategic bombers launched from Ukrainianka airfield, likely heading to Engels, reflecting strategic bomber deployment (Source: @eRadarrua/98842).
Ukrainian air defence systems engaged Shahed UAVs across multiple fronts, including central, northern, and southern sectors (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Ukrainian forces reported use of new cruise missiles Banderol and Dan, extending strike capability into Russian‑held territory (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31273).
Russian air strikes supported ground offensives in Sumy and Zaporozhye, with Su‑25 aircraft providing close air support (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).

key_developments: Reactive Shahed UAVs were detected approaching Kirovohrad, Dnipropetrovsk, and Chernihiv from multiple vectors, prompting intensified air‑defence alerts (Source: @eRadarrua/98822).
Ukraine fielded new Banderol and Dan cruise missiles with up to 500 km range and 150 kg warheads, enhancing deep‑strike options (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31273).
Over 50 Ukrainian drones were intercepted near Moscow during a night raid, indicating escalated aerial operations over Russian territory (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Four Tu‑95MS bombers took off from Ukrainianka airfield, likely en route to Engels, showing strategic bomber activity (Source: @eRadarrua/98842).
Intense ground combat continued in Sumy, with Su‑25 support and fighting in Bachivsk, Pisarevka, and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Ukrainian forces liberated Novoselovka in Zaporozhye region and advanced toward Yegorovka, pressuring Russian defensive lines (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31268).
Russian tactical aviation conducted strikes on southeastern and northern fronts, sustaining pressure on Ukrainian positions (Source: @kpszsu/66941).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian UAV and bomber activity, combined with intensified tactical aviation, poses a growing threat to Ukrainian airspace and ground forces across multiple fronts. Ukraine's introduction of Banderol and Dan cruise missiles expands its offensive reach, potentially altering the balance in contested zones. Continued Shahed attacks on central and southern cities strain civil defence and require sustained air‑defence resources.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is bolstering air‑defence networks and integrating new missile systems while seeking additional Western support, as evidenced by the Gripen deal and discussions on SCALP production. Russian logistical adjustments, such as closing border railway points, aim to consolidate supply lines amid heightened NATO scrutiny. Neighboring states remain vigilant, with Poland monitoring cross‑border protests and Russia reinforcing its western frontiers.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of reactive UAV flight paths indicates coordinated launch sites in occupied territories, necessitating targeted counter‑UAV operations. The deployment of new cruise missiles suggests a shift toward longer‑range precision strikes, requiring updated threat libraries. Russian bomber movements from Ukrainianka warrant surveillance of strategic air corridors to anticipate potential deep‑strike missions.
